clinical_history: The following ECG from an elderly woman shows all of the following except:
diagnosis: c) Sinus rhythm (about 90/min) is present with alternating 3:2 AV Wenkebach and 2:1 block, not blocked premature atrial complexes (PACs).  Both heart block and blocked PACs can cause "group beating" due to the non-conducted P waves.  With AV block (Mobitz I or II), the sinus P waves should come "on time"; with blocked PACs, the premature P waves will come earlier than expected.   The ECG is suggestive of both proximal AV disease (3:2 AV Wenckebach pattern) and infranodal disease (right bundle branch block with borderline left axis deviation).  During subsequent electrophysiologic study at a time when pure 2:1 AV block was present, a Mobitz II mechanism was noted (HV interval 75 ms in conducted beats) and a dual-chamber pacemaker was implanted.
